name: Vía Verde La Maquinilla – circular between Belmez and Peñarroya
name_original: Vía Verde La Maquinilla – circular entre Belmez y Peñarroya
region_intro: Jogging around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo offers a blend of natural landscapes and historical elements. The municipality is situated in the province of Córdoba, at the northern end of the Sierra Morena, providing a scenic backdrop for running. The area features prominent natural features like El Peñón, along with local parks and green spaces suitable for various running routes. Its unique landscape is also shaped by a rich mining heritage, offering distinctive views for runners. Best jogging routes around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo The most popular jogging route is Vía Verde La Maquinilla – circular between Belmez and Peñarroya, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This route follows a former railway line, offering a relatively flat and scenic path. Another top favourite among local runners is Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This route provides a circuit around the town, combining urban and natural scenery. Local runners also love the Running loop from Belmez, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through local green spaces, often completed in about 29 minutes. Jogging around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo is defined by Sierra Morena foothills, historic mining landscapes, and accessible green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy park runs to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. These include paths through local green spaces, urban circuits, and trails along former railway lines.

Q: Are there easy running paths suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
A: Yes, for those looking for an easier run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Belmez&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 2.9-mile (4.7 km) trail leads through local green spaces and is generally completed in under 30 minutes, making it ideal for a relaxed jog.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: The terrain varies, offering a blend of natural landscapes and historical elements. You'll find relatively flat paths, such as those following former railway lines, as well as routes with moderate elevation changes, especially around features like El Peñón. The area's mining heritage also adds unique visual elements to some trails.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000543633/via-verde-la-maquinilla-circular-between-belmez-and-penarroya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde La Maquinilla – circular between Belmez and Peñarroya&lt;/a&gt;, which is a longer 9.4-mile (15.1 km) trail. For a shorter loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt;, which combines urban and natural scenery.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while running?
A: The region is rich in history and natural beauty. You might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views. The area's mining heritage is also visible, with features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of Mining&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the prominent natural feature of El Peñón provides a scenic backdrop for many routes.

Q: Are the running trails in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo suitable for families?
A: Many of the routes, especially the easier and moderate ones, can be enjoyed by families. Paths through local parks and green spaces, or the flatter sections of the Vía Verde, are generally suitable for a family outing. Always check the route's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural trails and greenways in Spain are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or specific route details for any restrictions before heading out with your canine companion.

Q: Where can I park when accessing the running routes?
A: Parking availability will depend on the specific starting point of your chosen route. For routes originating within Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo or Belmez, you can typically find street parking or designated public parking areas within the towns. For trails like the Vía Verde, look for access points near the towns, which often have small parking facilities.

Q: What makes running in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo unique?
A: Running in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo offers a unique blend of experiences. You'll jog through the foothills of the Sierra Morena, encounter landscapes shaped by a rich mining history, and pass by charming local towns. The combination of natural beauty and historical context provides a distinctive and engaging backdrop for your run.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging in Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to run year-round, though you should be prepared for cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a greater challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle – Sierra Boyera Reservoir loop from río Guadiato&lt;/a&gt; offer a more demanding experience. This route spans over 13.6 miles (22 km) and includes significant elevation changes, providing a robust workout amidst scenic landscapes.
tips:
Belmez Castle is a historic fortress located in Belmez. It stands majestically on a high limestone rock, offering a stunning view of the village and surrounding areas.
The castle dates back to at least 1245, with significant additions made in the 15th century. It played an important role during the Reconquista and was occupied by French troops during the Peninsular War (1810-1812). The castle features a pentagonal Keep, six semicircular towers and a zigzag staircase leading up to it.
If you're planning a visit, it's open daily and admission is free.

Once upon a time, there was a dog here known as "El Terrible" because of its ferocity. That dog is also credited with finding the first coal deposits in the area, around which a town arose, on the outskirts of Peñarroya, which was called Pueblonuevo El Terrible in honor of the feared dog to which they owed so much. Decades later, Peñarroya and Pueblonuevo joined forces, losing the dog's name along the way, but not forgetting him as they built a statue in his honor.

waypoints.name: Statue of El Terrible Dog, Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo
